articles.title: LINKEDIN DEEPENS VIDEO AD PUSH, TAPS MORE PUBLISHERS AND CREATORS TO SPUR GROWTH
articles.summary: LinkedIn is expanding its BrandLink program by adding more publishers and creator-led video shows, with sponsors including AT&T Business, IBM, SAP, and ServiceNow. Since launching, it has brought in over 70 publishers and creators, with the number of creators on the platform nearly doubling since 2021. BrandLink revenue rose nearly 200% in the three months to June 30 compared to the prior quarter, while publisher and creator payouts more than tripled year over year. Video remains one of LinkedIn's fastest-growing formats, with uploads up over 20% by July and views rising 36% year over year as of February.

articles.title: NETWORKS, NOT AI OR SEARCH, ARE THE #1 TRUSTED SOURCE AMID INFORMATION OVERLOAD
articles.summary: LinkedIn research shows professionals trust their networks more than AI or search. 43% cited colleagues as their top source for advice, and 64% said networks help them decide faster. Rising AI expectations overwhelm workers, as 51% feel learning AI is like taking on another job. For brands, this means B2B buyers rely on peer insight as much as company channels.

articles.title: GLOBAL MARKETING LEADERS SAY THEY'LL INVEST MORE IN SOCIAL MEDIA
articles.summary: 80% of marketing leaders plan to shift funds to social media as it becomes central to customer acquisition, loyalty, and revenue. Top platforms driving business impact are Facebook (70%), YouTube (68%), and TikTok (64%). 87% of leaders will increase paid social spend, over 80% will raise investment in influencer and organic social, and 75% plan to grow social team headcount, especially in SEO roles. However, only 44% rate their teams as “expert” in measuring ROI, which is often blamed on a lack of sophisticated reporting.

articles.title: DISCOUNT COUPONS: WHEN TO GIVE % OFF VS $ AMOUNT OFF
articles.summary: Percentage coupons are more effective for store-wide promotions than fixed amount discounts. In an analysis of over 70,000 grocery orders, percentage coupons led to 42% more customer spending, 56% larger basket sizes, and 50% more profitability compared to fixed amount coupons. Fixed amount coupons work better for discounts on specific products or brands because they are easier for customers to calculate. Very large discounts, such as 60%, can hurt sales if customers doubt product quality.

articles.title: ETHOS IN ADVERTISING: WHY IT WORKS + EXAMPLES
articles.summary: 81% of consumers say they need to trust a brand before buying, but only 39% trust advertising overall, creating both a challenge and an opportunity for businesses. Ethos, which focuses on credibility, builds trust by showing character, competence, and care. Small businesses can use strategies like testimonials, community engagement, founder highlights, celebrity or influencer endorsements, and awards to establish credibility in digital advertising. Credible ads lead to higher conversion rates, lower acquisition costs, and long-term customer loyalty. Unlike short-term discount ads, ethos-based marketing creates sustainable growth through trust and word-of-mouth.

articles.title: UNLOCK NEW LOYALTY FEATURES IN GOOGLE ADS & MERCHANT CENTER
articles.summary: Google Ads is introducing new loyalty offerings for brands. Retailers can now personalize member-only pricing and shipping benefits across both free and paid listings on Google, designed to drive deeper engagement with the most valuable shoppers. Sephora has used personalized annotations to show signed-in shoppers specific discounts based on their loyalty tier, which resulted in a 20% increase in click-through rate for personalized ads shown to loyal customers. Google Ads is also launching a loyalty goal to help optimize budgets towards the highest-value shoppers.

articles.title: ZUBTITLE
articles.summary: Zubtitle streamlines video editing for social media by automatically transcribing speech and adding captions, headlines, and progress bars. It also enables resizing and formatting for various social platforms.

articles.title: SOP: AI-ACCELERATED WRITING
articles.summary: This “smart chain” turns complex topics into clear content through 7 steps. These include agent research, voice dictation, synthesis, examples, metaphor, polish, and micro-test with iteration. It provides ready prompts, quality checks, and deliverables, enabling end-to-end management of the content process.

articles.title: PRIVACY-SAVVY, KID-SAFE: THE NEW PLAYBOOK FOR KIDS' ADS ON CTV
articles.summary: Kids' content and co-viewing households are very valuable for advertisers since children influence household purchases. However, strict privacy laws like COPPA and GDPR-K, combined with the growth of ad-supported and FAST platforms, make compliance a major challenge. Regulators judge campaigns by likely audience, not genre, so brands must replace data-driven targeting with context-based strategies, creative suitability, and data minimization.

articles.title: A CHEEKY PINT WITH ANTHROPIC CEO DARIO AMODEI
articles.summary: In this interview, Dario Amodei, CEO of Anthropic, discusses how he built the company with his sister and six other co-founders. He covers their work with the DoD, how the team proved skeptics wrong on revenue, and how AI models display capitalistic impulses as they improve. He also explains the current AI market structure and players, AI advancements vs. safety regulations, and how he personally uses AI.

articles.title: WILL THIS CONTENT WORK?
articles.summary: 5 questions to ask if your website content is problem-led or playbook-led.

articles.title: 9 WAYS TO IMPROVE AN AD
articles.summary: Fred Manley critiques VW's famous "Think Small" ad, arguing that cleverness replaced clarity.
